# RustyNES v2.0.7 — "Harbor" ("Trim")

**iOS polish + the App Store submission floor.** The seventh release of the v2.0.x "Harbor" mobile-finalization train, and the third of the four-release **iOS finalization window (v2.0.5 → v2.0.8)**. It runs on the byte-identical **v2.0.0 "Timebase"** core — **AccuracyCoin 141/141 (100.00%)**, nestest 0-diff, the `#![no_std]` chip stack untouched — so **no accuracy, save-state, or determinism number moves**. Host / iOS-only.

This is the polish-and-readiness cut ahead of the v2.0.8 iOS RC: it wires the App Store build-SDK floor, reconciles the deployment target with the code, and re-audits the privacy manifest.

---

## The core is unchanged (141/141)

Nothing in the emulation core changed since v2.0.3. AccuracyCoin holds **141/141 (100.00%, RAM-authoritative)**, nestest is 0-diff.

## App Store submission floor wired (iOS 26 SDK / Xcode 26)

From **2026-04-28**, Apple requires every App Store Connect upload to be **built with the iOS 26 SDK (Xcode 26)** — this is the *build* SDK, not the minimum OS (the deployment target may stay lower). The tag-gated iOS CI (`.github/workflows/ios.yml`) now **selects the newest `Xcode_26*.app` on the runner** before building the xcframework, and **falls back with a warning** (never a hard failure) on an older runner image, so the compile-validation job keeps running everywhere while the release runner uses Xcode 26. Source: Apple Developer "Upcoming Requirements" — <https://developer.apple.com/news/upcoming-requirements/?id=02032026a>.

## Deployment target reconciled (iOS 15.0 → 17.0)

`ios/project.yml` declared `iOS 15.0`, but the SwiftUI shell already uses **`NavigationStack`** (iOS 16) and, at **12+ unguarded sites**, **`ToolbarItem(placement: .topBarTrailing)`** (iOS 17) — so a real 15.0 build was never possible; 15.0 was an aspirational declaration, not a supported floor. v2.0.7 sets both the `options` and target-level deployment targets to **iOS 17.0** to match the code's actual API floor.

> **Product note for the maintainer:** this is the **minimum OS** the app runs on. If a lower floor (iOS 15/16) is intended, the `.topBarTrailing` / `NavigationStack` usages must first be availability-guarded (or `.topBarTrailing` → `.navigationBarTrailing`); v2.0.7 only makes the declaration honest against the code as it stands.

## Privacy manifest re-audited (no change needed)

`PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy` was re-audited against the **v2.0.6 crash reporter**: it collects **no new data type** and adds **no new required-reason API**. It is off by default; when on it writes **local** files (excluded from iCloud / device backups) and uploads nothing, so no `NSPrivacyCollectedDataType` applies; its only required-reason API is **UserDefaults** (the opt-in flag), already declared as `CA92.1`. It does not read file timestamps, disk space, boot time, or active keyboards. The manifest therefore needs **no change** — the audit outcome is recorded inline in the manifest header.

## Performance / energy notes (on-device deferred)

The Metal present path (nearest-blit → the WGSL shader stack) and CADisplayLink ProMotion pacing were reviewed at the source level; the app-thinning / on-demand-resources posture and the Instruments energy/thermal capture are inherently on-device and are staged for the v2.0.9 readiness pass. No host-side hot-path change was warranted (the core is untouched and byte-identical).

## Verification (host)

- **Core unchanged** — AccuracyCoin **141/141**, nestest 0-diff (not re-run; chip stack untouched).
- `cargo check --workspace` (version-only lock cascade) + `fmt` + `clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings` (no issues) + `RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings cargo doc` + the `no_std` cross-compile + both wasm32 clippy gates — all green.
- markdownlint (CI-pinned) clean; the `ios.yml` YAML change is additive + guarded.
- The Swift/Xcode build + on-device profiling remain the documented macOS / TestFlight carryover (no Xcode on the Linux host).

## Maintainer / on-device closeout (deferred to v2.0.9 / the TestFlight upload)

- Archive with **Xcode 26 / the iOS 26 SDK** and confirm the iOS-17 deployment target builds + runs on a real device.
- Instruments **energy / thermal** capture on ProMotion hardware; validate the app-thinning size posture.
- Re-run the v1.9.9 on-device TestFlight checklist.

## What's next — the rest of "Harbor"

- **v2.0.8** — the iOS release candidate (App Store Connect listing staged, TestFlight external beta, App-Review self-audit).
- **v2.0.9** — joint on-device readiness verification for both apps.
- **v2.1.0** — the **joint Google Play + Apple App Store + AltStore PAL + F-Droid launch**.
